properties.project_description: Restore hydrologic function and habitat on the 1,654-acre McCormack-Williamson Tract in a manner that also helps provide flood relief to the North Delta region by reconnecting the site as a floodplain to the Mokelumne River.

Habitat restoration effort in southern Sacramento County, near the confluence of the Cosumnes and Mokelumne Rivers, that will restore over 200 acres of floodplain and wetland habitats to support native fish and wildlife through hydrologic reconnection and native plant enhancement.
7 miles of setback levees in the Lower Elkhorn Basin along the east side of the Yolo Bypass.

Restoration of 176 acres of floodplain habitat by removing rock revetment, recontouring the riverbank, and converting agricultural land back to native riparian habitats, enhancing fish and wildlife habitat, floodplain function, and public recreation opportunities, while also reducing flood management costs.
Enhance floodplain habitat through improved connections to the Feather River and the Sacramento River.
Modify operations to create seasonal floodplain rearing habitat in the Tisdale and Sutter Bypasses.
Remove a fish passage impediment and install fish-protective screens in the Yolo Bypass.

properties.project_name: Prospect Island Tidal Habitat Restoration Project
properties.project_description: Restore daily tidal inundation across a diked historical marsh on Prospect Island (located within the Cache Slough Complex in Solano County) by breaching levees to reinstate tidal flow and enhance habitat for native fish, improve water quality, bolster climate resilience, and sequester carbon.
properties.display_name: Prospect Island Tidal Habitat Restoration Project

properties.project_description: Transformation of the Little Egbert Tract, a below-sea-level agricultural area at the southeastern end of the Yolo Bypass near Rio Vista, into enhanced habitat and flood conveyance zones by breaching existing levees, constructing subtidal swales and berms, and restoring native riparian and tidal ecosystems while also improving recreational opportunities and flood protection for nearby communities.

Restoration of approximately 2,200 acres at the downstream end of the Yolo Bypass in Yolo County to create a connected network of tidal marsh, floodplain, and wildlife-friendly agricultural habitatsbalancing flood management, ecosystem enhancement, and continued land use.

Converts approximately 1,200 acres of former grazing land near Oakley in the western SacramentoSan Joaquin Delta into a restored tidal marsh, re-establishing daily tidal flow to support native fish and wildlife, improve flood resilience, and serve as a living laboratory and community-accessed park.
properties.construction_completion_year_comments: Phase 1 completed 2021, now in Phase 2 (scheduled for completion in 2028).

properties.project_name: Feather River Salmonid Spawning Habitat Improvement - 2024
properties.project_description: The placement, sorting, and harvesting of gravel and cobble (1/4”-5”) to restore spawning habitat in the Feather River
properties.display_name: Feather River Salmonid Spawning Habitat Improvement - 2024

properties.project_name: P68 Feather River Salmon Habitat Improvement Project
properties.project_description: In 2023, the first phase of the P68 Feather River Salmon Habitat Improvement Project added approximately 8,300 cubic yards of optimal sized salmon spawning gravel at four locations in the Feather River between RM 63 to 67 to increase the amount of suitable and accessible spawning habitat for salmonids in the Feather River. Gravel will be added at other locations upstream of the locations that received gravel in 2023 when the second phase of the project is completed in 2026.
properties.display_name: P68 Feather River Salmon Habitat Improvement Project
